As of 2024, the top career paths are Data Science, AI/ML, Healthcare, Engineering, Law, Business Analytics and Cybersecurity
The general sub-streams in science are biology, physics, chemistry, computer science, biochemistry, biophysics, agriculture, environmental science, biotechnology, and many more.
The general sub-streams in arts are visual communication, journalism, interior design, English literature, psychology, sociology, foreign languages, film studies and many more.
The general sub-streams in commerce are accounting, economics, finance, information science, human resource management, tourism, business administration and many more.
The job opportunities in civil services are IAS, IPS, IFS, audit and accounting, postal service, armed forces, corporate law service and many more.
The branches in engineering are computer science, biotechnology, electronics and communication, chemical, civil, aerospace, electrical, bioinformatics, artificial intelligence and many more.

Yes, you can switch streams even after picking one. It's never too late. Take our personality test to find out what your strengths are!
It's not necessary to spend excessive time in any field to succeed. Your interests, strengths, and attitude towards learning is what matters the most.
The only way to choose a fulfilling career is to explore a wide spectrum of choices. All disciplines are interconnected. Start with what you love!
Data Science is one of the most in-demand careers today. It involves analyzing large datasets to derive insights and support decision-making.
Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning are revolutionizing every industry. Careers in AI/ML offer excellent growth and salary prospects.
Cybersecurity is a rapidly growing field. As digital threats increase, organizations need skilled security professionals to protect their systems.
Engineering is a versatile field with branches like Computer Science, Mechanical, Civil, Electrical, Chemical and many more. Each has excellent career prospects.
Medicine and Healthcare offer highly rewarding careers. Options include MBBS, BDS, Pharmacy, Physiotherapy, Nursing and many allied health sciences.
Law is a prestigious career path. You can specialize in corporate law, criminal law, civil law, international law, intellectual property law and many more.
Commerce graduates have great opportunities in banking, finance, accounting, management, economics and entrepreneurship.
The humanities stream opens doors to careers in journalism, social work, teaching, psychology, public administration and creative arts.
Pursuing an MBA after graduation can open up leadership and managerial roles across all industries.
Entrepreneurship is a great career option if you are passionate, risk-tolerant and have innovative ideas. Many successful startups began with young entrepreneurs.
To pursue engineering, you need to appear for JEE Main and JEE Advanced in India. For medical, NEET is the primary entrance exam.
For studying abroad, you need to prepare for exams like GRE, GMAT, IELTS or TOEFL depending on the country and course.
Scholarships are available from many organizations including government bodies, universities, and private foundations. Research early and apply on time.
Internships are extremely valuable. They give you real-world experience, help build your network, and often lead to full-time job offers.
Soft skills like communication, teamwork, problem-solving and adaptability are as important as technical knowledge in today's job market.
The salary in IT sector ranges from Rs 3 LPA for freshers to over Rs 40 LPA for experienced professionals depending on skills and company.
Finance and Banking sector offers competitive salaries. Fresh graduates typically earn between Rs 3-6 LPA with growth potential over time.
Healthcare professionals earn well in India. Doctors can earn Rs 6-20 LPA while specialists earn significantly more.
The test will help identify your strengths and match them with the best-suited career options based on your personality.
You can retake the test anytime. The more honestly you answer, the more accurate your results will be.
Career counseling helps students make informed decisions about their future. It considers your interests, aptitude, values, and market trends.
Vocational training is an excellent option for those who prefer skill-based learning. It leads to quick employment in trades like electrician, plumber, beautician, chef and more.
Digital Marketing is a rapidly growing field. Skills in SEO, content marketing, social media, and analytics are highly sought after.
Graphic Design and UI/UX Design are creative careers with excellent demand in the digital age. Tools like Figma, Adobe XD and Photoshop are essential.
Teaching and education are noble and stable career paths. You can teach at schools, colleges, or online platforms.
Social work and NGO careers are fulfilling options for those who want to make a difference in society.
Sports management, coaching and sports science are emerging career paths for those passionate about athletics.
Animation, gaming and visual effects are booming creative industries offering exciting career prospects.
Environmental science and sustainability careers are growing as the world focuses on climate change and green energy.
Psychology offers careers in clinical psychology, counseling, organizational behavior, research and many more areas.
Fashion design is a competitive but rewarding career for those with creativity and passion for the fashion industry.
Hotel management and hospitality offer global career opportunities in hotels, airlines, event management and tourism.
Defense services including Army, Navy and Air Force offer prestigious and secure career options for disciplined candidates.
Architecture combines creativity and engineering. It requires clearing NATA exam in India and offers fulfilling creative careers.
Chartered Accountancy (CA) is one of the most respected finance qualifications in India, offering excellent career prospects.
Company Secretary (CS) is a rewarding career in corporate governance, legal compliance and strategic management.
